themes.heading: Latest update
themes.text: Still current at:20 July 2026 Updated:20 July 2026 Latest update:We've reviewed our advice for Saudi Arabia and have raised the overall travel advice level. We now advise 'reconsider your need to travel' overall due the security situation in Saudi Arabia and the region. Military strikes and reprisal attacks have occurred in a number of locations in the Middle East in recent days, including directed towards targets in Saudi Arabia. This includes Abha International Airport. Avoid military bases and energy infrastructure. Prioritise your safety, monitor events and follow local advice. If strikes occur, move to an enclosed shelter. If these are not available, seek an inner room without windows. Military conflict in the region may result in widespread movement restrictions, airspace closures, flight cancellations and other travel disruptions. Terrorism is a threat, including in Saudi Arabia. Terrorist attacks could happen at any time or anywhere. Maintain a high level of security awareness. Avoid possible targets including venues that draw in foreigners, tourists or crowds where possible. If there's a security incident, follow the advice of local authorities (see 'Safety'). We advise: Reconsider your need to travel to Saudi Arabia overall due to the unpredictable security situation in Saudi Arabia and the region. Higher levels apply in some areas. Download Saudi Arabia Download Middle East

themes.heading: Middle East conflict
themes.text: Conflict in the Middle East region has escalated in recent days, with military strikes and reprisal attacks occurring in a number of locations across the region. Prioritise your safety, monitor events and follow local advice - including any warnings to take shelter or avoid locations at high risk of targeting. Conflict in the region may cause flight disruptions and disrupt your travel plans. Airspace closures and flight cancellations can occur with limited notice. The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has issued advice to air operators to avoid Gulf airspace until at least 29 July 2026. You should check with your travel provider to confirm if your flights are affected. If you travel anywhere with a risk of armed conflict, you may be unable to leave if conflict escalates further. ‘Do not travel’ means ‘do not transit’. ‘Reconsider your need to travel’ also means ‘reconsider your need to transit’. If you need to travel to any level 3 locations, stay as short a time as possible and eliminate unnecessary activities. Advice levels can go up at short notice if conditions deteriorate. For more information, see our page on the Travel and security impacts of conflict in the Middle East and read our travel advice below.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: We now advise 'reconsider your need to travel' overall due the unpredictable security situation in Saudi Arabia and the region. Military strikes and reprisal attacks have occurred in a number of locations in the Middle East in recent days, including directed towards targets in Saudi Arabia. This includes Abha International Airport. Avoid military bases and energy infrastructure. Prioritise your safety, monitor events and follow local advice. If strikes occur, move to an enclosed shelter. If these are not available, seek an inner room without windows. Military conflict in the region may result in widespread movement restrictions, airspace closures, flight cancellations and other travel disruptions. Check with your airline or tour operator before travel. Monitor developments closely and follow the advice of local authorities. Stay aware of your environment. Terrorism is a threat, including in Saudi Arabia. Terrorist attacks could happen at any time or anywhere. Avoid possible targets including venues that draw in foreigners, tourists or crowds where possible. Maintain a high level of security awareness. If there's a security incident, follow the advice of local authorities.There may be increased risk of attached on US interests globally. Demonstrations and protests are illegal in Saudi Arabia and there may be severe penalties for participation. Avoid all demonstrations and protests should they occur. Crime rates are low, but theft can happen, especially in crowded places and from vehicles. Physical, verbal and sexual harassment can also occur. Be prepared for extreme weather during the hottest months, between June and September when temperatures can exceed 50˚C. Sand and dust storms occur regularly. Follow advice of local authorities. Driving can be dangerous due to high speeds and reckless behaviour on the road. The annual road death toll is high. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Cases of Middle East Respiratory Syndrome (MERS) have been reported. Avoid contact with camels or products contaminated with camel secretions. Saudi Arabia has introduced travel bans and entry requirements for travellers who have recently visited Ebola-affected countries. Sandstorms and dust storms can cause allergies and breathing problems. Discuss your travel plans with a doctor if you have an existing condition. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Many laws in Saudi Arabia are different to those in Australia. If you’re detained or arrested, ask the Saudi authorities to inform the Australian Embassy in Riyadh. It’s illegal to mock, provoke or question public order, religious values or public morals. Criticising Saudi Arabia, the royal family, the leadership or Islam is also against the law. These laws extend to social media posts, even if posted many years ago and outside Saudi Arabia. Foreigners have been sentenced to decades in prison for offensive tweets made outside Saudi Arabia years before their visit to the country. Saudi Arabia has strict laws on relationships and LGBTQIA+ issues. It’s against the law for unmarried couples to live together or to have sex outside marriage. Public displays of affection between men and women or people of the same sex are also illegal. Punishment may include fines and/or imprisonment and the death penalty. Get professional advice for local legal matters. You may be prevented from leaving the country until issues are resolved. It’s illegal to possess, consume, produce or traffic drugs in Saudi Arabia. Penalties for drug offences are severe and can include imprisonment or the death penalty. It’s also illegal to buy, sell, import, carry and consume alcohol. The penalties are also severe and can include imprisonment. In Saudi Arabia, men and women should dress modestly in public. Cover your shoulders and knees and avoid tight-fitting clothes or clothes with profane language or images. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: For information on departing Saudi Arabia, see Travel. You can apply for a tourist visa online or on arrival. Check the Saudi eVisa website for current information on tourist visas. A visa does not guarantee your entry to Saudi Arabia. Check visa conditions before your arrival. Follow the advice of local authorities and remain aware of your surroundings at all times. All Saudi visa holders of Muslim faith are permitted to perform Umrah at any time throughout the year, except around Hajj time, when only Hajj visa/permit holders can enter Makkah.

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular help, contact the Australian Embassy in Riyadh. Follow the Embassy's social media channels for local information (see 'Local contacts'). Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. There have been several terrorist attacks in the Gulf region in recent years. These have occurred at places visited by foreigners. Take official warnings seriously. In case of an attack, follow the advice of local authorities. Maintain a high level of security awareness. Attacks could occur at any time and could target: public places, including shopping malls, markets, restaurants, hotels, recreational facilities and mosques military sites tourist sites, entertainment events and crowded public places embassies and consulates locations foreigners may visit If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. Read our general advice on protecting your safety during an armed conflict. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Military strikes and reprisal attacks have occurred in a number of locations in the Middle East in recent days, including directed towards targets in Saudi Arabia. Prioritise your safety, monitor events and follow local advice. If strikes occur move to an enclosed shelter. If these are not available, seek an inner room without windows. Military conflict in the region may cause travel disruptions including airspace closures and flight cancellations. There may be increased risk of attacks on US interests globally. Military tensions remain in neighbouring Yemen. Missiles, explosive-laden drones and explosive-laden boats have targeted civilian infrastructure in Saudi Arabia, including ports and airports. Most attacks have been intercepted by the Saudi military. Some of these attacks occur close to the Yemen border, especially in the provinces of Jazan, Najran and Aseer (including Abha). Urban centres in the Eastern Province have been targeted, in addition to oil infrastructure in that province. The capital Riyadh, and cities on the Red Sea, such as Jeddah and Yanbu, have also been targeted in the past. Other areas may be targeted from time to time. Targets have included: urban areas oil infrastructure military bases government facilities ports public facilities, such as airports Such attacks may continue. The frequency of attacks vary and not all individual attacks are always widely publicised. To stay safe in the event of an attack: if you hear a loud explosion or if sirens are activated, immediately seek cover stay away from windows or openings. seek shelter in the centre of the building at the lowest level possible be aware that if an incoming missile or drone is intercepted, falling debris can pose a risk after an attack, stay away from any debris follow the advice of local authorities and monitor the media for emerging threats

themes.heading: Demonstrations and protests
themes.text: Demonstrations are illegal, and any large spontaneous gatherings are unusual. You should avoid any demonstration or protest activity if it occurs. Penalties for participating in demonstrations or protests may be severe. More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping can happen anywhere, anytime, including in destinations that are typically at lower risk. The Australian Government’s longstanding policy is that it doesn’t make payments or concessions to kidnappers. More information: Kidnapping

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: The overall crime rate in Saudi Arabia is low. Petty crime can occur, including in crowded places, shopping malls and from vehicles. Protect yourself from crime: be alert to petty thieves while in crowded places carry only what you need – leave other valuables in a secure place keep your vehicle doors and accommodation locked take care when travelling outside major cities be alert to suspicious behaviour

themes.heading: Sexual assault
themes.text: If you are sexually assaulted, you may face detention, arrest or criminal prosecution for reporting the incident to local police or when seeking medical help. If you're a victim of sexual assault, contact the Australian Embassy in Riyadh on +966 11 2500 970, after hours calls will be redirected to the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre in Canberra: +61 2 6261 3305 (if calling from overseas) 1300 555 135 (if calling within Australia) Australian Embassy in Riyadh Consular officers can't provide legal or medical advice. However, they can provide lists of English-speaking service providers who may be able to help. See 'Local contacts'. If you're attending major entertainment events such as the MDLBEAST Music Festival, you may need to take extra precautions, particularly as a woman. Risks include: unwanted attention, groping and verbal sexual harassment sexual assault unsolicited invitations from strangers Protect yourself: dress conservatively in line with the Public Decency Laws, avoiding clothing that is tight-fitting or revealing be aware of your surroundings be cautious of unsolicited invitations from strangers only use licenced taxis or approved ride-share services, such as Uber, Bolt, or Jeeny and avoid drivers who approach you. More information: Sexual Assault Overseas Reducing the risk of sexual assault and harassment

themes.heading: Piracy
themes.text: Piracy occurs in the southern Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden. Pirates target all forms of shipping, including: commercial vessels pleasure craft luxury cruise liners naval vessels The International Maritime Bureau issues piracy reports. If you travel by boat to or near Saudi Arabia despite the risks: monitor piracy reports for the region exercise extreme caution be alert to possible threats avoid confrontation

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Saudi Arabia experiences extremely high temperatures June to August are the hottest months of the year. The temperature can reach over 50°C. Consume adequate liquids and avoid extended periods in the sun. Sandstorms and dust storms are common. If a natural disaster or severe weather occurs: secure your passport in a safe, waterproof location monitor the media and local sources follow the advice of local authorities keep in contact with your friends and family Register with the Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System to receive alerts on major disasters.

themes.heading: Tours and adventure activities
themes.text: Transport and tour operators don't always follow safety and maintenance standards. This includes adventure activities. If you plan to do a tour or adventure activity: check if your travel insurance policy covers it ask about and insist on minimum safety requirements always use available safety gear, such as life jackets or seatbelts

themes.heading: Swimming safety
themes.text: If proper safety equipment isn't available, use another provider. regularly check weather reports before engaging in water activities and take all warnings seriously stay informed by checking media and local sources for any potential dangers speak with your travel provider about safety equipment before continuing with your planned activities take a friend or family member along when swimming or participating in water activities ensure you have travel insurance that covers all planned activities be aware of any dress requirements and gender segregation rules. You may need to cover your arms and legs, or use different areas designated for men and women.

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance If you hold an eVisa for Saudi Arabia, check the health policy linked to your visa, as coverage may be limited. You should confirm the scope of your health insurance policy and any exclusions before you travel. For further information, check the Visit Saudi Official Website. If you hold a resident identity card (Iqama), your employer must provide medical insurance. Check your insurance policy to confirm what is and isn't covered. Your Saudi medical insurance will not cover pregnancy or maternity unless you're married and it's in your insurance policy.

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Ebola
themes.text: Saudi Arabia has introduced travel bans and entry requirements for travellers who have recently visited Ebola-affected countries. All entry to Saudi Arabia by those travelling from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Uganda and South Sudan is suspended until further notice. All visas are suspended for travellers arriving through a third country who have stayed in the DRC, Uganda and South Sudan within 21 days of their arrival. Travellers arriving from other countries at risk of Ebola outbreaks may be required to participate in health screenings at Saudi Arabian borders. Contact your nearest Saudi Embassy or Consulate for more information.

themes.heading: Air pollution
themes.text: Air quality in Saudi Arabia, particularly in major cities, can reach high pollution levels. Dust and sandstorms are severe and common. The poor air quality can cause: allergies and breathing problems traffic delays and accidents If you have pre-existing medical conditions, you may be affected. If you’re concerned: seek medical advice before leaving Australia monitor the air quality index reduce your exposure.

themes.heading: Insect-borne diseases
themes.text: Malaria is present in the Asir and Jizan areas, bordering Yemen. Cases of chloroquine-resistant strains of malaria have been reported. Other infectious diseases, including waterborne and foodborne diseases, are present in Saudi Arabia. Get medical advice if you have a fever, muscle pain, rash or severe headache. typhoid hepatitis brucellosis rabies dengue leishmaniasis bilharzia (schistosomiasis) Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical facilities
themes.text: There’s no agreement between Australia and Saudi Arabia for free healthcare. Tourists must pay for all medical care, even if they need to stay in hospital or be evacuated. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. Most facilities in major cities offer high to very high-quality services. Facilities in minor towns and small cities can only do routine procedures. If you're treated at a private facility, you may be expected to pay in advance before doctors will treat you. If you become seriously ill or injured, you may need to be evacuated to a place with better facilities. Medical evacuation can be very expensive. Facilities for travellers with a disability are different to Australia.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Possession of illicit drugs and alcohol is illegal. Penalties are severe. Convictions for possession and trafficking of drugs may result in heavy fines, lengthy jail sentences, deportation or the death penalty. If you're in the company of someone who is involved in drug cases, including trafficking, possessing or using drugs, you may be detained and remain in detention until investigations of all parties are concluded. It is illegal to bring alcohol into Saudi Arabia and to possess or consume it in the country. Public intoxication or driving under the influence of drugs or alcohol is punishable by fines, imprisonment, deportation and confiscation of your driving licence. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Resident identity card
themes.text: If you're a resident, you must always carry your resident identity card (Iqama). If you travel on a visa, you should always carry your passport. Saudi authorities have the right to check ID. There are security checkpoints, both in cities and on roads between cities.

themes.heading: Illegal items
themes.text: It's illegal to import, possess or trade in weapons and items held to be 'contrary to the beliefs of Islam'. Illegal items include: alcohol pork products pornography – including images of scantily clad people, particularly women political and religious books, comics and materials drones, without prior approval Authorities scan passenger luggage on arrival. More information: Saudi Customs

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ laws
themes.text: Same-sex relationships and impersonating the opposite sex are illegal. Saudi Arabia doesn't recognise or allow same-sex marriages and rights. Authorities can impose fines and severe punishment, including deportation. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Passports with 'X' gender identifier
themes.text: You will not be able to enter Saudi Arabia if your passport shows 'X' in the gender field.

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: Always respect religious and cultural sensitivities, rules and customs.

themes.heading: Ramadan
themes.text: The Islamic holy month of Ramadan is strictly observed in Saudi Arabia. Take care not to offend others and cultural customs during this time. It is illegal to eat, drink or smoke in public between sunrise and sunset during Ramadan. Authorities can detain and even deport you for doing so. Explore our Ramadan page to learn more, including dates for Ramadan.

themes.heading: Dress
themes.text: Get advice on what is acceptable clothing before you arrive. Both men and women are asked to dress modestly in public in line with Public Decency Laws. Avoid tight fitting clothing or clothes with profane language or images. Men and women visiting Saudi Arabia should cover their shoulders and knees in public. If you don’t comply with these requirements, you may face penalties. If approached by authorities, be respectful and remain calm. More information: Public decorum Advice for women

themes.heading: Departing Saudi Arabia
themes.text: Borders can open and close and entry and exit requirements can change without notice, especially during a conflict. Confirm requirements with local authorities. Check the latest information from local authorities on entry requirements and border arrangements. Plan for possible delays at border crossings. Make sure you have all documentation, and enough food, water and medication.

themes.heading: Kuwait
themes.text: The border crossing from Saudi Arabia into Kuwait is open. Only foreign nationals holding a valid Kuwaiti residency permit will be permitted entry. Australians intending to enter Kuwait should contact the Australian Embassy in Kuwait at least 24 hours prior to intended travel. The Embassy will share names and passport details with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to seek approval for entry. Travel should not proceed until you have received confirmation of entry being approved. More information: Travel advice for Kuwait

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Entry visa
themes.text: You need a valid visa to enter Saudi Arabia. You can apply for an e-visa at the Visit Saudi website. Be cautious of fraudulent visa links. Requirements and policies can change at short notice. Visit the Saudi eVisa website for current information on tourist visas. A visa does not guarantee your entry to Saudi Arabia. Check visa conditions prior to your arrival. If you've had a travel ban placed against you in the past to prevent you from re-entering Saudi Arabia, you should check the duration of the ban prior to your arrival, or you may be denied entry to Saudi Arabia. If you have a previous unresolved legal case in Saudi Arabia, you may be arrested when you arrive. This is regardless of the passport you use to re-enter Saudi Arabia (whether you use an Australian or other passport). If you hold a tourist visa, it is valid for 12 months and allows multiple entries. You may stay up to 90 days during that period, and the visa cannot be extended or used for any form of work. For further information Visit Saudi Official Website. If you overstay your visa, you may face: a significant fine detention while waiting to be deported a ban from re-entering Saudi Arabia You won't be permitted to leave the country until the fine is paid in full. The Australian Embassy cannot waive or reduce fines or request the local authorities to do so. Tourists under the age of 18 may not be allowed to enter Saudi Arabia unless accompanied by a guardian. If you're visiting Saudi Arabia for purposes other than tourism, you must obtain a separate and special visa from visa agencies accredited to the Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia. Contact your nearest Saudi Embassy or Consulate. If you're transiting through Saudi Arabia, you may need a transit visa. Check with your airline, travel agency and your nearest Saudi Embassy/Consulate if you need a transit visa. Information is available on the Visit Saudi website. Only Muslims are allowed to enter Makkah (Mecca) and Madinah. You may not be allowed to enter through a land crossing if you hold a business/visit visa that is annotated 'via air'. Note that this may be written in Arabic. Carefully read and understand the restrictions of your Saudi business/visit visa. The 'Nusuk' platform can facilitate registration and visa processing for Hajj and Umrah pilgrims. This is the only approved platform from the Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah for Hajj visas and services. During the Hajj period, Saudi authorities may restrict entry to and exit from certain airports. Access to airports in Jeddah, Madinah, Yanbu and Taif for Hajj may be limited to travellers with a valid Hajj visa only. Pilgrims on Hajj visas are not permitted to travel around Saudi Arabia as tourists outside of their pilgrimage itinerary. Check with your airline, travel agency, and your nearest Saudi Embassy/Consulate for up-to-date information on entry and exit requirements during Hajj season. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest Embassy or consulate of Saudi Arabia for details about visas, currency, customs, vaccination and quarantine rules.

themes.heading: Exit visas
themes.text: If you hold a resident identity card (Iqama), you'll need to get an exit/re-entry or final exit permit from the Saudi Ministry of Interior. You must do this through your sponsor. You may not be eligible for an exit re-entry visa to leave Saudi Arabia if your passport has less than six months validity. If you're the main visa holder, make sure that all your dependants have valid passports, as you may not be allowed to obtain an exit re-entry visa. Visitors holding a single-entry or multiple-entry business, work or tourist visa don't need an exit permit to leave Saudi Arabia. If you're a dual national (e.g Australian/UK nationals), and you're travelling with both passports, you can only leave Saudi Arabia with the passport you used to enter.

themes.heading: Work visa
themes.text: It's illegal to work for anyone other than the sponsor or employer officially registered as your employer/sponsor on your residence permit in the Saudi Passports Office’s system. If you do, you may be punished with: fines detention deportation a ban from entering Saudi Arabia Holders of any type of visa other than a work visa or a resident identity card (Iqama) are not allowed to work in Saudi Arabia.

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter Saudi Arabia if you're travelling from an area with a risk of yellow fever. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever (PDF)

themes.heading: Travel bans
themes.text: Authorities may place you under a travel ban if you're a tourist, visitor or resident identity card (Iqama) holder and you're involved in a dispute about, but not limited to: business labour employment fines. Travel bans are strictly enforced, and they can take months or even years to resolve. Only Saudi Arabian authorities, and in very limited cases, your sponsor, can remove travel bans. Private Saudi citizens may also initiate travel bans against expats. The government may issue travel bans on people who: are charged with criminal offences are under investigation are involved in financial or labour disputes have unpaid debts or who have passed bad cheques

themes.heading: Travel restrictions on dependents
themes.text: Dependents up to the age of 21 of a Saudi Arabian parent need permission to leave Saudi Arabia. More information, visit the Absher platform. If you're an expat with unpaid dependent fees, you may have to pay the fees before you can: apply for an exit or re-entry visa renew your Iqama leave on your final exit.

themes.heading: Other passport conditions
themes.text: Saudi authorities will not allow you to enter Saudi Arabia on an Australian emergency passport. You can only use an Australian emergency passport to exit and you may be required to provide a police report and/or letter from the Embassy to explain why your passport is different to the passport on which you entered the country. If you obtain a new passport, including an emergency passport, while in Saudi Arabia to replace your old, lost or stolen passport, you must register the new passport number with the Saudi passport office prior to your departure. Check with your employer/sponsor or the Ministry of Tourism (depending on your visa type) for information on where to complete this process. Australian travel-related documents issued to non-Australian citizens will not be accepted for entry to Saudi Arabia with an electronic visa (even if you obtained the visa online prior to your arrival). You'll need to contact your nearest Saudi Embassy/Consulate for up-to-date advice and information on the acceptance of your travel document. If your entry into Saudi Arabia is denied despite holding a valid Saudi visa, the Embassy won’t be able to assist to facilitate your entry.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Saudi Arabian Riyal (SAR). Declare amounts of more than SAR60,000 or equivalent per family or group when you arrive or on departure. This covers all forms of currency, not only cash. Declare any precious metals worth more than SAR60,000 per family or group. You can change currency at commercial banks and exchange bureaus. ATMs are widely available. Ask your bank if your cards will work in Saudi Arabia. More information: Saudi Customs

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: Saudi Arabia has strict requirements for driver’s licences. If you’re a resident in Saudi Arabia, you'll need to get a Saudi driver’s licence. To drive in Saudi Arabia, you'll need: a valid Australian driver's licence (digital driver's licences may not be accepted) an International Driving Permit (IDP) You must get your IDP before departing Australia. Visitors to Saudi Arabia can only drive hire cars and not privately owned vehicles. You may only be allowed to use your Australian driving licence and IDP for up to 3 months from arrival in the country. Check with the local Traffic Police and your rental company for up-to-date information. You must carry your driver's licence and vehicle registration when driving. If you're involved in an accident, notify the traffic police (NAJM) and ask for their instructions.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: The annual death toll on Saudi roads is extremely high. Exercise extreme caution when driving and always wear a seat belt. Safety hazards include camels straying onto roads. There are checkpoints where you may be required to present identification. Some Saudi cities have an automated traffic ticketing system. If you're fined, you must pay the fine through this system before leaving the country. More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Taxis and rideshare services
themes.text: Only use registered rideshare services, taxis and limousines. Book transport from reliable a company. Registered public taxis can be in poor condition, often without meters and without seatbelts.

themes.heading: Public transport
themes.text: Saudi Arabia has inter-city bus and limited rail services throughout the country. Riyadh also has a metro system. Travellers often use limousine services for long-distance travel. More information: Transport and getting around safely

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: Due to military activity in Yemen, flights in the south of Saudi Arabia can be cancelled or rescheduled at short notice. Affected airports include: Jazan Abha Wadi Aldwasir Bisha Sharurah Najran Baha Check with your travel provider for updates on flights. DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Travelling with pets
themes.text: The Saudi Ministry of Environment, Water, and Agriculture must approve all pets imported into Saudi Arabia. Prior permit/approval to bring the pet/s must be obtained. Please contact your airline or visit the Saudia website and the Saudi Ministry of Environment, Water, and Agriculture for more information and requirements.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Contact the Australian Embassy in Riyadh for consular help.

themes.heading: Australian Embassy, Riyadh
themes.text: Diplomatic Quarter Riyadh Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Phone: +966 11 250 0900 Website: saudiarabia.embassy.gov.au/ryad/home X: @AusAmbKSA Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ausembksa Facebook: @AusEmbKSA LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ausembksa/ The working week is Sunday to Thursday. Check the Embassy website for details about opening hours and any temporary closures.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 대사관 연락처

themes.heading: 의료기관 연락처
themes.text: ㅇ 사우디아라비아는 비교적 우수한 보건 의료시설을 보유하고 있으며, 의료진은 대부분 제3국인 출신으로 영어 소통이 가능합니다. ㅇ 의료비가 고액이므로 가급적 보험에 가입하는 것이 바람직합니다. ※ 리야드 시내 종합 병원(한국인 간호사 근무 병원) - King Saud Medical Complex(공공병원으로 여행자의 경우, 응급처치만 가능), 전화: 435-5555 - King Fahd Hospital National Guard, 전화 : 011-801-1111 - Dr. Sulaiman Al Habib Hospital, 전화 : 011-283-3333, 490-9999, 525-9999 - Dallah Hospital (개인병원), 전화: 470-2777 ㅇ 주요 도시의 약국에서는 수입 약품들이 많이 구비되어 있으며, 약품 구입 시 처방전이 필요하나, 감기약, 진통제, 알레르기약 등 단순의약품은 처방전 없이도 구입이 가능합니다. ㅇ 리야드 시내 주요 약국(24시간 영업) - SAGAF(전화: 461-5921) - Al NAHDI(전화: 225-4388) ㅇ 메르스는 지역 별로 중점 관리 병원을 지정하고 있으므로, 의심증상 발생 시 혹은 인근 병원 전문 의료진을 통해 상담 및 진찰 가능합니다. - 리야드: Prince Mohammad bin Abdul-Aziz hospital (전화: 261-6666) - 동부: Dammam Medical Complex (전화: 846-7390) - 젯다: King Fahd Hospital (전화: 660-6111) - 전국: 메르스 무료 상담 전화 937 ㅇ 남서부 Jizan 지역은 말라리아 잠재 지역이며, 예멘 후티반군의 산발적 미사일 공격을 받고 있으므로 동 지역 여행 시 각별한 주의가 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 공식 국명 : 사우디아라비아 왕국(Kingdom of Saudi Arabia) ㅇ 위치 : 아라비아반도 중심(리야드 기준 북위 24도, 동경 46도)에 위치함 ㅇ 수도 : 리야드(Riyadh) ㅇ 면적 : 약 215만㎢(한반도의 약 10배) ㅇ 언어 : 아랍어(88.8%), 기타(11.2%) ㅇ 인종 : 아랍족(베두인족 27%, 기타 아랍 정착민 73%) ㅇ 종교 : 이슬람교(수니파 90%, 시아파 10%) ㅇ 기후 : 고온 건조한 대륙성기후, - 연평균 섭씨 32~34℃, 최고 54도, 최저 0도 ㅇ 시차 : GMT + 3시간(한국보다 6시간 늦음)

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고 현황
themes.text: □ 정정 상황 (전쟁·내란·테러 등) ㅇ 사우디아라비아는 보수적인 왕정 통치하에 조직적인 정치집단의 활동이 없으며, 기본적인 정치 상황은 안정된 것으로 평가됩니다. 또한 사우디 정부의 강력한 테러세력 척결 노력으로 치안이 다른 중동 국가에 비해 비교적 안정되어 있습니다. ㅇ 다만, '15년 3월부터 시작된 사우디를 주축으로 하는 연합군의 예멘 공습에 대한 예멘 반군 세력들의 반격, ISIL의 선동 등으로 인해 △사우디 정부군, △서구인(백인) △ 시아파 주거지역을 대상으로 하는 테러가 계속 발생하고 있습니다. 특히, 예멘 접경 지역인 지잔, 아시르, 나즈란 지역과 리야드, 젯다, 담맘 등 대도시의 주요 테러 대상 밀집 지역은 테러 재발 가능성이 있으므로 각별한 주의가 필요합니다. □ 치안상황(일반 범죄 등) ㅇ 전반적으로 치안상태는 양호한 편이나, 유색인종(노동자)에 대한 편견이 심하며 최근에는 소매치기와 차량절도 등이 빈번하게 발생하고 있으니, 차량 내에 주요 물품 보관을 가급적 삼가고 도심 외곽 등 인적이 드문 곳을 피하시는 게 좋습니다. ㅇ 최근 사우디아라비아 내 생활물가 급등 및 자국민고용우선정책으로 경제적으로 불안정한 제3국인이 증가, 금품을 노린 강력범죄가 증가하고 있습니다. 저소득 제3국인 밀집지역 및 도심 외곽 등의 방문을 삼가고 거액을 은행에서 인출 시에는 강도의 습격을 받지 않도록 대비할 필요가 있습니다.

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ㅇ 체류 기간 초과 문제 : 사업 목적으로 스폰서의 초청을 받아 방문하는 여행객이라도 여권 입국 도장에 기재된 체류기간(Duration of Stay)을 초과하는 경우 초청인(스폰서)은 15,000리얄(4,000미화 상당)을 벌금으로 내야하며 초과체류자는 구금될 수 있습니다. 따라서 방문 인사들은 입국 시 허가된 최장 연속 체류 기간을 반드시 확인하고 주의를 기울이는 것이 필요합니다. - 복수사증 방문자의 경우, 매 방문시마다 체류 기간(Duration of Stay)이 부여되며 이를 초과하지 않도록 각별한 주의를 요함. 비자의 유효기간(Validity)은 이를 국경(공항,육로,해로 등)에서 사용할 수 있는 기간을 의미하므로 체류기간(duration of stay)와 혼동하지 않도록 유의 ㅇ 차량 접촉 사고 등 : 대부분의 운전자들이 교통질서 의식이 미약하며, 과속 주행, 끼어들기 등 난폭운전이 횡행하여 도로상에서 차량 접촉 사고가 빈번히 발생하므로 차량 운전 시 방어운전 및 안전 운행 등 각별한 주의가 필요합니다. - 안쪽 차선은 과속 차량이 주행하므로 3차선 주행 시에는 가능한 중간 차선을 이용하는 것이 안전함(최우측 차선은 화물차량이 주로 주행). - 또한 교통사고가 발생한 경우, 경찰이 현장에 도착, 사고 조서를 작성할 때까지 사고 현장을 유지하는 것이 필수적이며, 상대방과 불필요한 논쟁을 삼가는 것이 바람직(교통경찰이 가해자와 피해자 및 책임 범위 결정). - 일정 기간(3개월간) 우리나라 국제운전면허 사용 및 렌터카 이용이 가능(단, 쌍방 보험 가입 여부 확인) 하나 단기 출장자의 경우, 가능한 운전기사가 포함된 렌터카 이용을 권장합니다. ㅇ 사진 촬영 문제 : 대부분의 공공기관 건물들이 보안구역으로 설정되어 사진촬영이 금지되어 있습니다. 또한 아바야를 착용한 여성들에 대한 사진촬영이 금기시되므로 공공장소에서 사진을 촬영할 경우에는 특별한 주의가 필요합니다. ㅇ 복장 불량 문제 : 사우디는 이슬람 전통을 유지하는 국가로 사우디 여성들이 외출하는 경우, 아바야(검은색의 이슬람 전통복장)를 착용토록 하고 있으나, 외국 여성의 경우 아바야 착용이 의무는 아니며 어깨와 무릎을 가리는 정도의 단정한 복장이 권고 됩니다. 또한 남녀를 불문하고 외출시 몸에 달라붙는 옷이나 이슬람을 모독하는 글, 그림이 있는 옷을 입어서는 안됩니다. - 경찰을 사칭한 범죄행위가 종종 발생하고 있으므로, 각별한 주의가 필요합니다. ㅇ 이슬람 규율 위반(주류, 돼지고기 등 금기 음식 및 음란물 반입 또는 소지) : 이슬람 규율에 반하는 주류, 돼지고기, 음란물 등의 반입이나 휴대는 엄격히 금지되며 발각될 경우 즉시 체포 및 실형에 처해질 수 있는바 수화물 탁송 등에 특별한 주의가 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 진도 3~5 규모의 지진이 년 1~2회 발생하나, 기록될 만한 피해는 없습니다. ㅇ 가끔 심한 먼지바람이 불어오는 경우가 있으며, 이러한 때에는 장거리 운전을 삼가는 것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 동절기에는 열대성 소나기가 내리는 경우가 있으며, 폭우가 내리면 도로가 침수되는 경우가 많으므로 폭우 시 운전은 피하는 것이 바람직하며, 낙뢰를 동반하는 경우에는 외출을 삼가는 것이 좋습니다. - 배수시설이 발달하지 못해 약간의 비에도 쉽게 도로가 일시적으로 침수되거나 범람하는 경우가 많음

themes.heading: 유의해야 할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 예멘 국경주인 지잔, 아시르, 나즈란 지역, 시아파 중심지역인 알-아흐사, 카티프 지역 등은 가급적 방문을 자제하시고 꼭 출장이 필요한 경우에만 적절한 안전조치를 하시고 방문하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 메르스 병원균의 매개체로 지목되고 있는 낙타와 접촉할 가능성이 있는 농장이나 우유가공공장, 그리고 메르스 격리병동 시설 등은 방문을 삼가시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 주요 도시에는 제3국 출신 근로자들이 다수 거주하며, 시내 중심가 이외의 외곽지역은 범죄 발생 가능성이 높으므로 가급적 저녁에는 단독으로 외곽지역을 방문하는 것은 자제하는 것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 리야드 중앙역, 시외버스 터미널, 바타 시장 등 사람이 많이 붐비는 곳은 소매치기에 의한 도난 사고가 많이 발생하고 있어 동 지역을 방문하는 경우에는 특별한 주의를 기울이거나 단독 행동은 자제하는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : O, (협정) 입국일부터 180일의 기간 내에서 90일 ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : O, (협정) 입국일부터 180일의 기간 내에서 90일 ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : X ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 도착사증 발급 - 수수료: SAR480($128.03) - 유효기간 1년 동안 총 90일 체류 가능 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 보수적인 이슬람국가 중 하나로, 종교와 풍습에 위배되는 금지품목(주류, 돼지고기 등) 반입 시 처벌 ㅇ 사우디 거주자(이까마 보유자)일 경우, 유효한 Exit-Reentry Visa 필요 ㅇ 4만 리얄(또는 이에 상당하는 외화) 이상의 현금·금괴·보석 등 소지 시 세관(Customs) 신고 의무 출국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 사우디 거주자(이까마 보유자)일 경우, 유효한 Exit-Reentry Visa 필요 ㅇ 4만 리얄(또는 이에 상당하는 외화) 이상의 현금·금괴·보석 등 소지 시 세관(Customs) 신고 의무 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : embassies.mofa.gov.sa/sites/SouthKorea/EN/Pages/default.aspx ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : overseas.mofa.go.kr/sa-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 : - 사우디 E-visa 안내 : visitsaudi.com/en - 사우디 비자센터 : vc.tasheer.com

themes.heading: 일반 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 관공서, 식당, 상점 등에서 일 처리가 다소 느리므로, 인내심을 가지고 기다리는 것이 필요합니다. - 경찰 등 관련 공무원들의 안내나 지시에 저항하는 경우, 예기치 못한 결과를 초래할 수 있으므로 주의가 필요합니다. - 특히, 라마단(금식월) 기간에는 단축근무가 실시되고 업무 처리가 사실상 정지되므로 미리 준비하는 것이 필요합니다. ㅇ 사우디는 공식적으로 이슬람 달력(히즈라력)을 사용하며 금요일, 토요일 관공서가 휴무하므로 유의할 필요가 있으며, 일반적인 근무 및 개점 시간은 다음과 같습니다. - 관공서 : 08:00-14:00 - 은행 : 09:30-16:30 ㅇ 돈이나 물건 등을 건네는 경우, 왼손을 사용하지 않도록 하며(이슬람교 영향), 한국인에게 호의적인 편입니다. 일반적으로 사진 촬영을 좋아하나 부정적으로 생각하는 사람들도 많이 있으므로 사전 허락을 받는 등 필요한 예의를 지키는 것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 관공서를 비롯한 군사·치안·산업 등의 주요 시설을 촬영하다 적발될 경우 처벌을 받게 되므로 주의가 필요합니다. ㅇ 운전을 급하게 하는 경향이 있어 신호위반이나 끼어들기 등 예기치 못한 상황이 자주 발생합니다. - 특히, 상당수의 재외국민 사망사고가 교통사고로 인한 것이므로 안전 운전에 각별한 유의 필요 ㅇ 전통문화 및 종교의 영향으로 외부 남성을 집으로 초대하는 것은 드문 편입니다. - 초대를 받았다면 현지인의 집에 방문할 수 있으며 환영의 의미로 제공하는 커피는 사양하지 않는 것이 예의입니다. - 보통은 남성과 여성의 공간이 분리되어 있으며, 상대방의 배우자 혹은 딸 등 여성에 대한 대화는 되도록 피하는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 종교 관련
themes.text: ㅇ 사우디아라비아는 이슬람 국가로서 와하비즘(이슬람 원리주의)이 실생활에 많은 영향을 미칩니다. - 무슬림 의무기도 시간(매일 5회) 중에는 일부 식당, 상점 등이 약 20-30분간 영업을 중단하거나 담당 직원이 자리를 비우는 경우가 있습니다. - 주류, 음란물, 타 종교 서적, 마약 등 소지가 금지되어 있으며 적발될 경우 엄하게 처리합니다. 특히 마약 소지 혐의로 발각되는 경우에는 극형에 처해질 수 있으므로 특별한 주의가 필요합니다. - 돼지고기는 이슬람 교리에 의해 판매 및 반입이 허용되지 않으며, 적발될 경우 처벌을 받을 수 있습니다. - 이슬람교 외에 다른 종교는 공식적으로 인정하지 않으며, 타종교 활동은 샤리아(이슬람 종교법)에 의해 활동을 인정하지 않습니다. 특히, 선교활동 적발 시엔 처벌 및 강제 추방 조치될 수 있음을 유의해야 합니다. ㅇ 사우디는 이슬람 전통을 유지하는 국가로 사우디 여성들은 아바야(검은색의 이슬람 전통 복장)를 착용하는 경우가 많습니다. 최근 들어서는 여성 아바야의 색상이 다양해지고 아바야를 착용하지 않는 여성도 증가하는 등 점차 변화하는 중입니다. - 외국 여성의 경우 아바야를 착용할 의무는 없습니다. 그러나, 과도한 노출을 지양하고 어깨와 무릎을 가리는 정도의 단정한 복장이 권고됩니다. - 모르는 여성에 대해서는 사진 촬영이나 말을 건네는 것이 금기시되어 있으므로 주의가 요구됩니다. ㅇ 과거 사우디아라비아 식당은 남성만을 위한 싱글석과 가족과 함께 온 여성을 위한 가족석으로 구분되어 있었으나, 2019년부터 싱글석/가족석 구분 없이 식당에 출입할 수 있습니다. 다만, 남녀분리를 금지하는 것은 아니어서 지방에 위치한 일부 식당은 여전히 남녀 좌석을 구분하기도 합니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 식당 영수증에는 부가세 15%가 포함되어 있으며, 식당에서 별도로 팁을 주는 경우는 드뭅니다. ㅇ 공항, 마트 등의 장소에서 제3국 근로자가 무거운 짐을 들어주는 경우 약간의 수고비를 주는 것이 일반적입니다.

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 신호체계, 일반법규 및 운전석 방향 등 대부분의 도로교통 표준이 한국과 동일하나, 일부 고속도로 구간의 주행 제한속도가 한국보다 빠르며(일부 구간 140km/h), 과속 및 난폭운전자들이 많습니다. ㅇ 유리에 짙은 선팅 필름을 부착한 것은 불법이며, 단속에서 적발될 경우 벌금(500~900리알, 한화 15만~27만원)이 부과됩니다. 또한, 경찰관이 짙은 선팅 필름을 제거하는 경우도 있습니다. ㅇ 주로 스피드 카메라를 이용하여 자동으로 속도를 측정하고, 규정 초과 시 속도 위반 단계별로 벌금이 차등 부과됩니다. 스피드 카메라를 이용하여 자동으로 속도를 측정하고, 규정 초과 시 속도 위반 단계별로 벌금이 차등 부과됨(2018.9.7. 개정) 과속 구간 제한속도 120km/h 이하 도로 제한속도 140km/h 도로 5km/h~9km/h - 300~500리알(한화 9~15만원) 10km/h~19km/h 150~300리알(한화 4.5~9만원) 800~1000리알(한화 24~30만원) 20km/h~29km/h 300~500리알(한화 9~15만원) 1200~1500리알(한화 36~45만원) 30km/h~39km/h 800~1000리알(한화 24~30만원) 1500~2000리알(한화 45~60만원) 40km/h~49km/h 1200~1500리알(한화 36~45만원) 50km/h 이상 1500~2000리알(한화 45~60만원) ㅇ 주요 교차로마다 신호위반 감시용 교통 카메라가 설치되어 있으며, 적색등에서 정차하지 않고 통과할 경우, 3,000~6,000리알(한화 90만원~180만원)의 벌금이 부과됨 ㅇ 앞좌석(조수석 포함) 안전띠 미착용 시 단속 대상임(뒷좌석 안전띠 착용 여부는 단속하지 않음)

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ A사 건설 현장의 한국인 직원 5명이 동부지역에서 인도인이 운전하는 SUV차량(도요타 세콰이어)을 타고 현장 숙소로 복귀하던 중 갑자기 도로상에 나타난 낙타와 충돌하여 차량이 전복되고 화재가 발생하여, 아국인 2명이 사망하고 3명이 부상당함 ㅇ B사 직원 C씨는 종교행사를 마치고 인도인 운전기사와 차량으로 현장으로 복귀하던 중 중앙선을 침범하여 마주 오는 픽업트럭과 정면충돌하여 C씨는 현장에서 사망하고 운전기사는 중상을 입음 ㅇ 강사 D씨는 동료들과 숙소에서 음주 후, 동료 1명과 차량을 운전하여 외출하던 중 불심검문을 받아 관할경찰서에 체포되어 구류되었고, 이후 법원에서 최종적으로 강제 추방 판결을 받음

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생 시 사고 현장을 최대한 그대로 보존한 채(가급적 사진으로 상세히 기록) 즉시 Najm(교통사고 손해보험협회, +966 9200-00560), 경찰(999) 및 긴급구조대(997)에 신고 ㅇ 교통사고 손해에 대한 보험처리를 위해서는 일반적으로 Najm(사우디 교통사고 보험협회)으로부터 사고기록 확인서를, 무보험차ㆍ불법체류자ㆍ범죄자 관련 교통사고는 경찰로부터 사고기록 확인서를 발급받아 본인 보험사에 제출해야 함 [대중교통] ㅇ 주요 도시 간 운행되는 장거리 시외버스가 있으나 국토가 넓어 원거리 여행 시에는 항공편을 이용하는 경우가 많습니다. 리야드-담맘, 리야드 후푸프, 리야드-압카이크 간에는 철도가 운행됩니다. 노선별 운임은 좌석 등급과 시간 등에 따라 차이가 납니다.(리야드-담맘(1등석 240리얄, 2등석 135리얄) 리야드-후푸프(1등석 200리얄, 2등석 95리얄) 리야드-압카이크(1등석 200리얄, 2등석 115리얄) ㅇ 버스 등 시내 대중교통수단이 발달하지 못한바 실제 한국인이 이용하는 경우는 드뭅니다. (리야드 시내 주요 구간 간 운행되는 버스가 있으나, 주로 제3국 근로자들이 이용하는 편) ㅇ 일반 택시보다 Uber, careem 등 스마트폰을 이용한 호출 서비스가 현지에서 더 선호되고 있습니다. 요금 시비가 발생할 가능성이 낮고, 이동 경로가 기록된다는 장점이 있습니다.(17년 5월 아국인을 대상으로 제3국인 택시강도 금품갈취 발생) ㅇ 택시는 기본요금이 10리얄이며, 미터기를 사용하지 않는 경우가 많아 택시 이용 전 요금을 미리 확인하는 것이 좋습니다. - 택시 운전기사는 대부분 제3국인으로 영어가 가능한 경우가 많습니다. - 리야드 공항에서 시내 중심(35킬로)까지 택시를 이용하는 경우 약 80리얄 정도 소요 [도로교통] ㅇ 사우디아라비아 도로는 양호한 편이나 우리나라와 달리 아스팔트 노면이 모래 등으로 인해 매우 미끄러워 급커브로 인한 사고가 빈발하고 특히 비가 오는 경우에는 특별한 주의가 필요합니다. - 주요 도시 간 고속도로의 경우 왕복 8차선임 ㅇ 고속도로에서 난폭 및 과속주행이 많아 차량 접촉 또는 전복 사고가 많이 발생하고 있습니다. 사우디는 교통질서를 강화하는 새로운 도로교통법을 2009년부터 시행, 교통질서 확립에 많은 노력을 기울이고 있습니다. ㅇ 고속도로 및 주요 간선도로에 단속 감시카메라가 설치되어 있으며 과속 위반에 대한 벌금을 지불하지 않은 경우 차량 매매나 출국이 불가능합니다.(과속, 신호위반 등에 따라 300리얄에서 6,000리얄까지의 벌금과 함께 벌점제를 병행하여 누적 시 면허 취소 등이 될 수 있음) - 고속도로 상한 속도 : 구간에 따라 120킬로 혹은 140킬로 가변. 주행 시 속도표지판 확인 필요 ㅇ 교통사고로 인명피해가 발생한 경우, 모든 당사자는 경찰에 출두, 책임 여부를 가려야 하며, 사망자 발생 시 1인당 최대 20만 리얄 상당의 배상액을 물어야 하므로 보험 가입 시 이를 반영하여야 합니다. ㅇ 시내 횡단보도에 보행자가 있어도 차량을 질주하는 경우가 많으므로 횡단보도를 건너는 경우에도 주변을 살피는 것이 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 사막을 통과하는 고속도로 이용 시 낙타 및 가축과 충돌하여 대형 교통사고로 이어지는 경우가 있으므로, 전방 야생동물보호 또는 동물출현 표지판을 주시하고 항시 규정 속도를 준수해야 함 ㅇ 환절기 모래폭풍이나 짙은 연무로 전방 시야 확보가 어려운 경우가 있으며, 야간 고속도로 주행 시 가로등이 없는 경우가 빈번하므로 운전 시 각별한 주의가 필요함 ㅇ 종교적 사유로 음주 자체가 불법이므로, 음주운전 시 한국보다 훨씬 엄격한 법규로 처벌받음(적발 시 구류 및 벌금 납부 후, 강제 추방) ㅇ 테러 및 안보 위협 대비 목적으로 주요 고속도로 구간마다 검문소가 설치되어 있으며, 해당 검문소 통과 시 전조등을 끄고 감속한 후 경찰관의 지시에 응해야 함

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 사우디는 한반도의 약 10배에 달하는 광대한 영토에 사막성 기후이며, 지역에 따라 기후 차이가 나기도 합니다. 대체로 리야드가 소재한 내륙지역은 열대 사막 기후로 고온 건조하며, 해안지역(젯다 등 홍해 연안지역, 담만 등 걸프 연안지역)은 고온다습한 기후입니다. - 연평균 기온은 30-40℃이나, 한여름에는 기온이 종종 50℃까지 올라가기도 하며, 겨울철에는 기온이 10℃이하로도 내려감 ㅇ 계절이 바뀌는 시기(11-12월, 3-4월) 사막 모래바람이 자주 불어 호흡기 질환 및 눈병에 걸릴 가능성이 높으므로 주의가 필요하며, 특히 리야드는 매우 건조하므로 피부 관리에도 주의하는 것이 좋습니다. - 동절기에는 야간에 체감온도가 크게 떨어지므로 동절기 여행 시에는 방한 의복을 준비하는 것이 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: □ 차량 수리 ㅇ 사고 차량을 수리하는 경우 교통사고 기록 확인서(Najm report 또는 Police report)가 있어야 하므로, 사고 발생 시에는 반드시 Najm(교통사고 손해보험협회, +966 9200-00560) 또는 경찰(999)을 불러 즉시 사고 조서를 발부받아야 합니다. ㅇ 사고 차량 견인은 경찰의 협조를 받아 개별적으로 연락해야 합니다. □ 전화 및 우편제도 ㅇ 리야드 시내에는 공중전화가 없으므로 가급적 휴대전화를 로밍하여 휴대하거나 미리 현지 SIM카드를 준비하는 것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 사우디에서 우리나라로 국제전화를 사용하는 경우 전화 요금은 분당 약 4리얄입니다. ㅇ 사우디에서 우리나라로 우편물을 보내는 경우의 요금은 다음과 같습니다. - 일반우편 : 113리얄(500 그램 이하) - 등기우편 : 165리얄(500 그램 이하) - 우체국 택배 : 189 리얄 - DHL 요금 : 약 360 리얄 □ 한국과의 시차 ㅇ -6 (한국보다 6시간 늦음) □ 전기 제품 사용 ㅇ 전기 사정은 대체로 양호하며 220v용 접속구(UK 방식)가 설치되어 있습니다. 가정용 요금은 저렴한 편이나 누진제가 적용되어 상용 전기료는 비싼 편입니다. □ 언어 ㅇ 아랍어가 국어이나 제3국인이 다수 거주하는 리야드, 젯다, 담맘 등 대도시는 영어로 소통 가능한 사람이 많이 있습니다. ㅇ 다만, 관공서에서는 아랍어 문서만 취급하며 영어로 의사소통이 되지 않는 경우가 자주 있으므로, 아랍어로 대화 가능한 동행인과 함께 방문하는 것이 편리합니다.

themes.heading: Country summary
themes.text: There were no changes to the advisory level or risk indicators. Advisory summary was updated. Reconsider travel to Saudi Arabia due to risk of Iranian drone and missile targeting of American interests, armed conflict, terrorism, exit bans, and local laws regarding social media activity. Some areas have increased risk. Read the entire Travel Advisory. Do Not Travel to the Yemen border due to threat of Terrorism. Advisory summary On March 8, 2026, the Department of State ordered non-emergency U.S. government employees and U.S. government employee family members to leave Saudi Arabia due to safety risks. This amends the prior on March 3, 2026, Department of State authorization order for non-emergency U.S. government employees and U.S. government employee family members to leave Saudi Arabia due to safety risks. Armed conflict Following the onset of hostilities between the United States and Iran on February 28, there has been an ongoing threat of drone and missile attacks from Iran. Commercial flights are operational from Saudi Arabia though have been significantly disrupted. The U.S. government has limited ability to offer emergency services to U.S. citizens in Saudi Arabia due to the safety risks. U.S. government employee travel restrictions near Yemen U.S. government employees working in Saudi Arabia are not allowed to travel within 20 miles of the Yemen border due to safety risks. U.S. government employees are not allowed to travel to Qatif for non-official travel due to safety risks. The U.S. government has limited ability to offer emergency services to U.S. citizens in the Yemen border region due to the safety risks. U.S. citizens in Saudi Arabia are advised to take the same precautions. Terrorism There is risk of terrorist violence, including terrorist attacks in Saudi Arabia. Visit the U.S. Department of State’s country reports on terrorism to learn more. Terrorism is a concern in Saudi Arabia. Attacks can occur with little or no warning, especially around holidays. They can involve knives, firearms, and vehicles. Potential targets include: Important political, cultural, or religious locations Places visited by U.S. citizens and other foreigners Tourist locations and large gatherings Hotels, and locations where American citizens gather Transportation hubs Markets and shopping malls Entertainment venues Government facilities Terrorists may use violence to respond to direct attacks against them or other military operations in the region. Missile and drone attacks from Iran and threats from Houthis in Yemen. Iran has launched missile and drone attacks into Saudi Arabia. Attacks have targeted cities, infrastructure, airports, military bases, diplomatic, and energy facilities throughout the country. Iran-backed Houthi rebels have threatened to attack Saudi Arabia again. Falling debris from intercepted drones and missiles are also a risk. U.S. citizens living and working near energy infrastructure and military bases may be at increased risk, especially closer to the Yemen border. Exercise increased caution due to exit bans U.S. citizens have been prevented from leaving Saudi Arabia. Exit bans may be related to pending criminal and civil investigations and cases, including, but not limited to unpaid visa overstay fees, domestic family disputes, and other financial and labor disagreements. Exit bans prevent individuals from leaving the country. These can last for many years. In some cases, exit bans can continue even after a criminal sentence has been fully served or a private suit is settled. Exercise increased caution due to local laws Saudi authorities may detain you if they consider your social media posts or other online activity to be critical, offensive, or disruptive to the public order. U.S. citizens have been arrested for past social media activity, including comments posted while outside of Saudi Arabia. This may include posting, re-posting, or liking comments critical of the Saudi government or its leaders and policies. This also may apply to comments about public life or religion in Saudi Arabia. Local laws against cybercrime and terrorism may be used to prosecute cases related to social media use. Punishment for social media activity has included prison sentences of up to 45 years. Local authorities may not take into account when, where, or in what context posts are made. You are subject to local laws when traveling in Saudi Arabia. If you break laws in Saudi Arabia – even by mistake – you could be deported, fined, imprisoned, or subject to an exit ban. Some U.S. citizens have been detained for several months or even years without charges. Aviation safety The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has issued a Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) or a Special Federal Aviation Regulation (SFAR). This is due to risks to civil aviation operating within or nearby the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman region, including Saudi Arabia. Consult the Federal Aviation Administration for the most up to date information on current FAA warnings. Risks in specific areas Level: 4 – Do not travel The Yemen border region Do Not Travel to the Yemen border due to threat of Terrorism. Armed groups in Yemen have attacked Saudi border towns and other sites in Saudi Arabia with armed drones, missiles, and rockets. People near the border with Yemen are at increased risk. U.S. government employees working in Saudi Arabia are not allowed to travel within 20 miles of the Yemen border due to safety risks. Terrorist groups continue to plan possible attacks in Saudi Arabia, including Qatif. U.S. government employees working in Saudi Arabia are not allowed to travel to Qatif for non-essential travel due to safety risks. U.S. government employees working in Saudi Arabia need special authorization to travel to Qatif due to safety risks. The U.S. government has limited ability to offer emergency services to U.S. citizens in these areas due to safety risks. U.S. citizens in Saudi Arabia are advised to take the same precautions. For U.S. citizens in Saudi Arabia: Enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to get important updates and alerts from the U.S. embassy or consulate. Enrolling helps the U.S. embassy or consulate contact you or your emergency contact in an emergency. Monitor local media for breaking events and be prepared to adjust your plans. Many prescription medications need prior approval before bringing into or taking out of Saudi Arabia. Before you travel, register any listed prescriptions at the Controlled Drug System (CDS) website. This approval process may take several weeks. Review our information on Terrorism and travel to High-Risk Areas. Have a plan to leave in an emergency that does not depend on U.S. government help. Review our information on Crisis Response and Evacuations. Keep travel documents up to date and easy to access. Stay aware of your surroundings. Stay alert in large crowds or tourist spots. Avoid public displays of affection, particularly between same-sex couples. Visit Saudi Arabia’s website for information about public decency regulations. Do not carry, produce, use, or distribute illegal drugs in Saudi Arabia. Review local laws and conditions before traveling, including our Saudi Arabia country information on arrests. Review our information for Gay and Lesbian travelers. Before you travel, delete any sensitive photos, comments, or past content that might be seen as controversial or inappropriate in Saudi Arabia. Do not post, share, or like such content while in Saudi Arabia. Do this for your social media accounts, cameras, laptops, phones, and other accounts and devices. Visit our page regarding Arrest or Detention Abroad for more information on what happens when you or a loved one are arrested abroad. Review the Country Security Report for Saudi Arabia. Visit the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) page for the latest Travel Health Information for Saudi Arabia. We highly recommend that you buy travel insurance before you travel. Check with your travel insurance provider about evacuation assistance, medical insurance, and trip cancelation coverage. Whether you’re a first time or frequent traveler, use the International Travel Checklist.

themes.heading: Regionale Risiken
themes.text: Die USA und der Iran haben am 17. Juni 2026 eine Absichtserklärung zur Beendigung des Konflikts unterzeichnet. Die Lage im Nahen und Mittleren Osten ist weiterhin angespannt. Eine rasche Verschlechterung der Sicherheitslage ist jederzeit möglich. Mit kurzfristigen Luftraumsperrungen und Flugausfällen muss gerechnet werden. Meiden Sie Orte in der Nähe von potenziellen Zielen wie z.B. diplomatische, militärische und sicherheitsrelevante Infrastruktur. Halten Sie sich über die regionale Lageentwicklung auf dem Laufenden und befolgen Sie die Anweisungen der lokalen Behörden.
